pick.rationale: Kentucky enters with a slight analytical edge at 28th in KenPom and 32nd in Torvik, but significant injury concerns loom with point guard Jaland Lowe (season-ending shoulder surgery) and guard Kam Williams (broken foot) both out, leaving the Wildcats shorthanded in the backcourt. Santa Clara boasts an elite offense ranking 23rd in adjusted offensive efficiency—actually better than Kentucky's 39th—and reached the WCC tournament final where they led #12 Gonzaga for 23 minutes before fading, showing they can compete with tournament-caliber competition. The Broncos' 26-8 record and 15th-ranked offense give them a credible upset path, but Kentucky's 32nd-ranked defense, SEC-tested pedigree (9th-toughest schedule), and strong performances from Otega Oweh and Denzel Aberdeen in the SEC tournament provide just enough cushion to survive a dangerous 7/10 matchup against a depleted roster.

That’s one game better than they were through seven SEC games last year. After Saturday’s 72-63 win over Ole Miss at Rupp Arena, Kentucky head coach Mark Pope gave an update on Kam Williams and Jaland Lowe, both of whom need surgery to repair their injuries. Williams sustained a broken foot in Wednesday’s win over Texas. He had surgery on Friday, and it’s unclear how long exactly he will be out. There is hope he may be able to come back, potentially in March, but the timeline is still uncertain. Lowe has been out since going down against Mississippi State. He’s on his way to have surgery, which is why he wasn’t on the UK bench, and is expected to miss around six months. That means he could potentially return sometime during summer workouts, but at worst, should be ready for the start of fall camp. Kentucky is now 14-6 (5-2, SEC) and will travel to Nashville to take on Vanderbilt on Tuesday night, before a massive road game at Arkansas next Saturday night. After Saturday’s 72-63 win over Ole Miss at Rupp Arena, Kentucky head coach Mark Pope gave an update on Kam Williams and Jaland Lowe, both of whom need surgery to repair their injuries. Williams sustained a broken foot in Wednesday’s win over Texas. He had surgery on Friday, and it’s unclear how long exactly he will be out. There is hope he may be able to come back, potentially in March, but the timeline is still uncertain. Lowe has been out since going down against Mississippi State. He’s on his way to have surgery, which is why he wasn’t on the UK bench, and is expected to miss around six months. That means he could potentially return sometime during summer workouts, but at worst, should be ready for the start of fall camp. Kentucky is now 14-6 (5-2, SEC) and will travel to Nashville to take on Vanderbilt on Tuesday night, before a massive road game at Arkansas next Saturday night.
